_meta.description: Condition catalog for Mutants & Masterminds 4e. All 'effect' fields are original, short paraphrases written for this project, not taken from the rulebook. Mechanical fields (category, supersedes, includes, recovery) reflect game rules/facts, not copyrighted prose.

categoryKey.condition: A general status effect, imposed by an effect, action, or circumstance
categoryKey.damage_condition: Specifically imposed by failing a Damage resistance check, part of the damage ladder
categoryKey.object_damage_condition: The object/equipment equivalent of a damage condition

_meta.supersedesNote: If a character already has a condition and gains one that supersedes it, the new (more severe) condition replaces it rather than stacking.
_meta.includesNote: Some conditions are shorthand bundles of several simpler conditions at once, listed in 'includes'.

conditions.name: Asleep
conditions.effect: Unconscious. Wakes on a strong Perception check, sudden movement, or a noticeable effect.

conditions.name: Blind
conditions.effect: Can't see; also counts as Sight Unaware and Vulnerable, plus impaired for sight-dependent tasks.
conditions.recovery: Depends on the cause; permanent if it's a fixed trait.

conditions.name: Bound
conditions.effect: Physically tied up or restrained solidly enough to stop movement and struggling.

conditions.name: Broken
conditions.effect: An object no longer functions.

conditions.name: Compelled
conditions.effect: Forced to take a single standard action per turn chosen by whoever compelled you.
conditions.recovery: Depends on the cause; Controlled supersedes this.

conditions.name: Confused
conditions.effect: Acts unpredictably each turn — may attack the source of confusion, act normally, do nothing, flee in fear, or lash out randomly.

conditions.name: Controlled
conditions.effect: Another character dictates your actions each turn, though you keep your normal capabilities.

conditions.name: Dazed
conditions.effect: Limited to free actions plus one standard action per round; can't take reactions.
conditions.recovery: DC 10 Fortitude check at the end of the next turn (damage-caused); other causes may need a different check.

conditions.name: Dead
conditions.effect: Life has ended; no further recovery checks apply.

conditions.name: Deaf
conditions.effect: Can't hear; counts as Hearing Unaware and is easier to catch off guard by sound.

conditions.name: Debilitated
conditions.effect: Has effectively lost the use of a specific trait.

conditions.name: Defenseless
conditions.effect: Defense treated as 0; automatically fails Dodge resistance checks and can be hit with a routine attack.

conditions.name: Delusion
conditions.effect: Highest degree of Hallucinating — the subject's whole perceived reality can be fabricated.

conditions.name: Destroyed
conditions.effect: An object is completely wrecked, in pieces or fragments.

conditions.name: Disabled
conditions.effect: -5 penalty on all checks, or on a specific named category of checks.

conditions.name: Dying
conditions.effect: Incapacitated and at serious risk of death; makes a stabilization check each turn.
conditions.recovery: Stamina check to stabilize (self) or a Treatment check from another character. See mm-actions-adventure.md §3.

conditions.name: Exhausted
conditions.effect: Worn out from overexertion — reduced effectiveness and half speed.
conditions.recovery: About 4 hours of rest, dropping back to Fatigued.

conditions.name: Fatigued
conditions.effect: Tired from exertion — minor penalties and reduced speed.

conditions.name: Figment
conditions.effect: Mildest degree of Hallucinating — small, believable changes to perception.

conditions.name: Frightened
conditions.effect: -5 penalty on checks related to the source of fear, and can't willingly approach it.

conditions.name: Hallucinating
conditions.effect: Perceiving things that aren't real; severity ranges from Figment to Phantasm to Delusion.

conditions.name: Hindered
conditions.effect: Movement speed halved.

conditions.name: Hit
conditions.effect: Minor damage taken; stacking -1 penalty on further Damage resistance checks per Hit.
conditions.recovery: DC 10 Stamina check once per minute removes one Hit.

conditions.name: Immobile
conditions.effect: Can't move from your current spot, though you can still act.

conditions.name: Impaired
conditions.effect: -2 penalty on checks, either all of them or a named category.

conditions.name: Incapacitated
conditions.effect: Down and out of the fight, usually knocked prone as well.
conditions.recovery: DC 10 Stamina check once per minute.

conditions.name: Paralyzed
conditions.effect: Frozen and unable to act physically, though still mentally aware and able to take purely mental actions.

conditions.name: Phantasm
conditions.effect: Middle degree of Hallucinating — larger, more complex false perceptions.

conditions.name: Prone
conditions.effect: Lying down — penalty on close attacks made, bonus for close attacks against you, penalty for ranged attacks against you, and reduced ground speed.

conditions.name: Restrained
conditions.effect: Held or bound; becomes Immobile instead of Hindered if anchored to something fixed.

conditions.name: Staggered
conditions.effect: Badly hurt — limited actions and reduced speed.
conditions.recovery: DC 10 Stamina check once per minute (if no worse condition is also present).

conditions.name: Stunned
conditions.effect: Can't take any action at all, including free actions and reactions; Dodge rank halved.
conditions.recovery: DC 10 Fortitude check at the end of the next turn (damage-caused); other causes may need a different check.

conditions.name: Surprised
conditions.effect: Caught completely off guard; normally lasts just one round.
conditions.recovery: Automatic after the round it's applied.

conditions.name: Susceptible
conditions.effect: Penalty on resistance checks against a specific effect or descriptor, equal to half that effect's rank.
conditions.recovery: Depends on the cause; often a fixed trait.

conditions.name: Transformed
conditions.effect: Some or all of the character's traits are altered, sometimes drastically.

conditions.name: Unaware
conditions.effect: Oblivious to your surroundings, or to a specific sense if named (e.g. Sight Unaware); fails related Perception checks automatically.

conditions.name: Vulnerable
conditions.effect: Defense and Dodge ranks are halved (round down).

conditions.name: Weakness
conditions.effect: Penalty on resistance checks against a specific effect, and the best possible outcome is capped at one degree of failure.
conditions.recovery: Usually a fixed trait; not something that clears in play.
